_comment: Golden vectors for the VoiceAttack return-channel wire protocol (ADR-0006, frozen in M1). Shared by the Python parser/serializer (src/vaivox/infrastructure/voiceattack/protocol.py) and the C# plugin (M4) so the two implementations never drift. 'round_trip' vectors are produced by build_reply AND parsed by parse_match_outcome (byte-exact serialization both sides must reproduce). 'parse_only' vectors exercise the defensive parser on degraded/forward-compat input; reply_bytes there is NOT a build_reply output. reply_bytes includes the trailing newline. expected_matched/expected_resolved describe the MatchOutcome parse_match_outcome must return; null means the parser returns None (unknown).

parse_only.name: empty_string_resolved_command
parse_only._note: An empty-string resolved_command is a valid string and preserved as-is (distinct from null); the parser does not coerce it.

parse_only.name: forward_compat_unknown_field
parse_only._note: Unknown fields (latency_ms) are ignored; a newer plugin can add fields without breaking older readers.

parse_only.name: no_trailing_newline
parse_only._note: Parser tolerates a missing trailing newline (framing strips/ignores it).

parse_only.name: non_boolean_matched
parse_only._note: Non-boolean 'matched' (string) -> None (unknown); the field must be a JSON boolean.

parse_only.name: non_string_resolved_command_coerced_to_null
parse_only._note: A non-string resolved_command is coerced to null while matched stays honored (best-effort).
